title: Systems Inventory
text_content:
The Universal Short-Form Virality Bible
An expert manual for solo creators in any niche—beginner to advanced

Layer 1 — Core Mechanics (Universal Systems)
1) Strategy Foundations
Why this matters: Strategy turns scattered posts into a repeatable growth engine.
1.1 Universal Topics (big doors)
Audiences consistently engage with broad human problems/desires—use these as entry points:
Mental & Emotional: overthinking, confidence, belonging, stress, discipline
Practical & Skills: productivity, money, career, creativity, study/learning
Lifestyle & Identity: health, fitness, beauty, relationships, parenting, culture
Curiosity & Entertainment: hacks, myths, challenges, transformations, behind-the-scenes
1.2 Niche Anchoring Formula
Universal → Niche → Framework
Example: “Stress” → “first-year teachers” → “2-minute classroom reset.”
Example: “Confidence” → “freelance designers” → “3-line client intro script.”
1.3 Content Thesis (write once; guide everything)
“I help [avatar] who struggle with [pains] achieve [dream outcomes] using [frameworks/systems].”
Exercises
Brainstorm 20 universal entry points; pick 3–5 pillars.
Write 1 thesis; say it out loud—trim until clear and specific.
✅ Checklist
3–5 universal topics chosen
Each anchored to your niche with a named framework
Thesis statement finalized
🚫 Common Mistakes
Topics too narrow or jargony
No clear who/what/how
Copying others’ pillars instead of mapping to your offer

2) Hooks (the first 1–3 seconds)
Why this matters: 80% of outcomes are decided before second 3.
2.1 High-performing archetypes
Identity Mirror: “If you’re a new nurse…”
Binary/Polarizing: “99% of people ruin their resumes by…”
Authority Claim: “It took me 10 years—here’s the 60-second version.”
Pain Trigger: “If your anxiety spikes at night, listen to this.”
Myth-Busting: “They told you to do X. That’s wrong.”
2.2 Expanded hook vault (edit brackets)
Educational: “Here’s exactly how to [OUTCOME] in 3 steps.” “If you don’t have [X], do [Y] instead.”
Opinion/Polarizing: “Stop [HABIT] if you actually want [RESULT].” “Hot take: [OPINION].”
Lifestyle/Story: “Day 1 of starting over my entire life.” “X years ago I was [STRUGGLING]—this changed everything.”
Pattern-Break: “This is [#] of [ITEM].” “Your life is boring because you don’t [ACTION].”
Aspirational/Data: “How I saved $X in 90 days.” “82% fail from this one mistake.”
2.3 Hook SOP
Draft 10 hooks per theme
Record 3 takes per hook (tone & wording)
Post 2–3 variants over 7–10 days
Keep the winner (highest 3-sec hold / impressions)
✅ Checklist
30+ hooks drafted across pillars
At least 3 recorded takes per hook
2–3 variants posted & measured
🚫 Common Mistakes
“Hey guys…” or dead air first second
Weak/unclear promise
Hook doesn’t match the rest of the video

3) Script Frameworks
Why this matters: Structure = retention.
3.1 Snap Script (15–30s)
Hook (0–3s) → Pain (3–10s) → 1-Line Shift (10–18s) → Binary CTA (18–30s) Example: “Stop planning workouts. You waste 20 minutes. Do 2 sets of 5 moves. Comment ‘PLAN’ for my split.”
3.2 Story Script (60–120s)
Hook → Pain → Conflict → Epiphany → Framework → Proof → CTA Use for deeper teaching or soft sales.
3.3 Objection-Buster (≤45s)
Call out belief → Reframe → 1 proof → CTA Example: “No experience? Rephrase bullets as outcomes… Comment ‘CV’ for my template.”
3.4 Long Framework (complete)
Hook (0–5) → Context (5–20) → Conflict (20–30) → Epiphany (30–60) → Framework (60–90) → Proof (90–110) → CTA (110–120)
✅ Checklist
Every line adds one new idea
Cut 20% words after read-through
Add 1 humor OR vulnerability beat
🚫 Common Mistakes
Rambling; burying the lead
Over-explaining steps; no “aha” moment
Skipping proof

4) CTAs & Offer Integration
Why this matters: Attention without direction = waste.
4.1 CTA types
Follow: “Follow for more [topic] playbooks.”
Engagement: “Comment ‘X’,” “Tag a friend,” “Save this for later.”
Sales: “I help [avatar] go from [pain] → [result] in [time]. Comment ‘INFO’.”
4.2 Identity-Bridge Formula
“Be the person who [action] today → comment ‘[word]’ / save / follow.”
4.3 Offer mapping (solo-friendly)
Top of funnel: save/comment prompts; micro-wins
Middle: lead magnet/DM keyword (one freebie you always use)
Bottom: main offer (one core product/service)
✅ Checklist
Exactly one CTA per video
CTA aligns to ToF/MoF/BoF goal
Series mapped to lead magnet/offer
🚫 Common Mistakes
No CTA or asking for 3 things at once
Vague CTAs (“Let me know…”)
Misaligned CTA (sales ask on purely inspirational clip)

Layer 2 — Cinematic Craft (Retention Systems)
5) Cinematic Shooting
Why this matters: Clarity and visual rhythm increase watch time.
Settings (universal)
24fps (cinematic) / 60fps (slow-mo accents)
Shutter: 1/48 or 1/50
ISO: as low as possible
Profile: Neutral/Flat (for gentle grading)
Focal length psychology
24mm: expansive but distant
35–50mm: natural, personal (best for talking head)
85mm+: dramatic isolation
Framing
Center-weighted; lead eye centered
Keep text above bottom 15% (UI safe zone)
Add depth (foreground: plants, frames, glass)
Shot sequencing
Wide → Mid → Close → POV (for movement across locations)
B-roll ideas
Hidden reveals (fridge, shelf)
Chest-mounted POV (immersive)
Macro (hands, objects)
Car rigs (hood, wheel-level, trunk, cabin)
✅ Checklist
Correct fps/shutter; clean audio
Wide/mid/close variety
At least 1 B-roll insert
🚫 Common Mistakes
One static angle; poor lighting
Micro-shakes without intention
Covering captions with UI bars

6) Editing & Retention
Why this matters: Short-form is pace-sensitive.
Pacing & pattern
Cut every 0.7–1.5s
Pattern break every 3–5s (zoom, angle change, overlay, subtitle punch)
Captions & on-screen text
≤1 sentence per card; 5–7 words
Bold/caps for keywords; high contrast
Title (top), subtext (mid), captions (bottom safe zone)
Audio
Voice louder than music by ~6–9 dB
Light de-ess; noise gate
Sync cuts to beats/gestures
Proof overlays (selling)
Screenshots, before/after, receipts, graphs
✅ Checklist
Hook visible/audible at 0–1s
Filler removed; breaths tightened
Pattern breaks every ≤5s
Captions legible; keywords emphasized
🚫 Common Mistakes
Long pauses; text walls; tiny fonts
Muffled voice; music too loud
No visual variety

7) Retention Curve Deep-Dive
Why this matters: Your retention graph tells you exactly what to fix.
Typical dips & fixes
1–3s drop: hook too soft → add identity callout, prop, motion on frame 1
10–15s dip: setup drag → compress context, cut a sentence, add b-roll
25–35s dip: over-explaining → deliver “aha” sooner; merge steps
40–50s dip: flat tone → inject humor/vulnerability; contrast edit
End drop: weak CTA → binary ask + open-loop tease for next video
Process
Review retention weekly
Annotate timestamps; patch; respin with change highlighted
✅ Checklist
Annotated dips with likely cause
Patched version published within a week
Result logged (improved or not)
🚫 Common Mistakes
Ignoring analytics; blaming the topic
Changing 5 things at once (can’t learn what worked)

8) Thumbnails & First Frames
Why this matters: Thumbnails entice; first frames hook.
Thumbnails (Reels/Shorts)
Expressive face + prop/action
≤4 bold words; high contrast; clean background
First frames (TikTok/Reels)
Start mid-action or with a visual jolt
Display hook text immediately; no dead air
Movement or pattern-break on frame 1 (zoom/pan/prop)
✅ Checklist
Custom thumbnail saved when platform allows
First word in first second; matching on-screen text
Freeze-frame looks good as thumbnail
🚫 Common Mistakes
Relying on auto-thumbnail
Starting seated & still with no text
Cramped text; low contrast

Layer 3 — Growth & Scaling (Systems)
9) Content Pillars & Series
Why this matters: Pillars build identity; series build loyalty and habit.
Pillars (pick 3–5)
Mindset & Self-Mastery • Health/Fitness • Money/Business
Creativity/Skills • Lifestyle/Identity • Authority/Proof
Polarizing Opinions • Culture/Entertainment
Series formats
Weekly arcs (e.g., “Overthinking Week”)
Identity spines (“If you’re a junior dev…”)
“Day 1” challenges/reset arcs
“1-Minute Playbooks” (repeatable quick frameworks)
✅ Checklist
Pillars chosen and documented
1–2 recurring series named
Each series mapped to CTA/offer
🚫 Common Mistakes
Random content with no spine
Abandoning a series after 1–2 episodes

10) Multi-Episode Storytelling Arcs
Why this matters: Arcs create bingeability and repeat viewers.
Arc types
Transformation: Day 1 → Day 30
Challenge: 30 days of [habit]
Mystery: “The mistake that almost ruined me…” (Part 1 → reveal later)
FAQ: Sequential answers to common questions
Arc SOP
Plan 3–5 parts before filming
Use consistent hook phrasing with part numbers
End each part with an open loop (“tomorrow I’ll show…”)
Publish daily or alternate days; pin playlist
✅ Checklist
3–5 episode outlines written
Open-loop lines drafted
Playlist pinned after Part 2
🚫 Common Mistakes
Inconsistent cadence (kills momentum)
No loop to pull viewers to next episode

11) Lifecycle & Recycling (Volume Moat)
Why this matters: Winners are assets—multiply them.
5–10 respin ideas for a winner
New hook (identity vs proof vs myth-bust)
New POV (first-person vs voiceover)
New format (talking head → VO + b-roll)
New CTA (save vs comment keyword)
New caption/title angle
Add proof overlays or remove them for pace
Slow-mo or speed-up cuts; different music bed
PODS method (from long content)
Pain → Objection → Decision → Step (+ Proof + CTA)
✅ Checklist
Top 3 winners identified monthly
Each respun 5+ ways
Performance logged; best variant kept
🚫 Common Mistakes
Treating videos as one-offs
Only posting fresh ideas; never respinning winners

12) Distribution
Platform nuance
TikTok: testbed; experimental pacing; trends
Reels: polished aesthetic; prioritize saves/shares
Shorts: tutorials/how-tos; playlist threading; clear titles
Cadence
Beginner: 3–4/week
Advanced: 1–2/day
Cross-post
Keep core video; adjust captions/thumbnail style per platform
✅ Checklist
Posted to 2–3 platforms
Caption & thumbnail adapted
Comments monitored early (reply to double threads)
🚫 Common Mistakes
Copy-pasting captions across platforms
Ignoring early-comment replies (huge for momentum)

13) Metrics & Troubleshooting
Track the right 5
Hook Hold: 3-sec views / impressions
Avg Watch % (and average view duration)
Comment Rate: comments / views
Share Velocity: shares per 100 views (early window)
Save Rate: saves / views
Troubleshooting Matrix
Low 3-sec hold: strengthen hook; add motion/text on frame 1
Low Avg Watch %: faster cuts; pattern breaks; compress setup
Low comments: binary CTA; sharper opinion
Low shares: clearer value; surprising insights; “send to a friend” line
Low saves: add template/cheatsheet value + explicit save prompt
✅ Checklist
Weekly review of top/bottom 3 videos
One targeted fix per underperformer
Notes logged for future scripts
🚫 Common Mistakes
Chasing only views/likes (vanity)
Changing too many variables per test

14) Storyboards & Case Studies (teaching examples)
Snap (Fitness)
Hook: Talking head mid-motion—“Overthinking is killing your workouts.”
Pain: B-roll scrolling 5 apps; overlay “20 mins wasted.”
Shift: Close—“Do 2 sets, 5 moves. Leave.”
CTA: Title card “Comment ‘PLAN’ for my split.”
Objection-Buster (Career)
Hook: “If your resume says ‘responsible for,’ you’re invisible.”
Reframe: “Use outcomes not duties.”
Proof: Overlay bullet rewrite before/after.
CTA: “Comment ‘CV’ for template.”
Arc (Business)
Part 1: Dark room—“The one mistake that almost bankrupted me…”
Part 2: Screenshots/receipts as overlays; VO explains
Part 3: Outdoor bright—“3 rules that fixed it”; proof metric; CTA to free guide

Appendices — Fast Action Tools
A) Pre-Record Checklist
Topic → Niche angle → Named framework
3 hooks drafted (identity/binary/authority)
Script chosen (Snap/Story/Objection-Buster)
Shot list (wide/mid/close/POV + one b-roll)
CTA aligned to funnel stage
B) Edit Checklist
Hook in ≤1s; no dead air
Cut every 0.7–1.5s; pattern breaks ≤5s
Captions high-contrast; keywords emphasized
Voice +6–9 dB over music
CTA end card + pinned comment text ready
C) Metrics Tracker (columns to log)
Date • Platform • Pillar • Hook text • Format • Length
3-sec hold % • Avg watch % • Comments/100 views • Shares/100 views • Saves/100 views
Outcome (Winner/Retry/Kill) • Respin Ideas
